[ch11_craft_001] PASS if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:145 still conveys the lane, closing officers, waiting carriage, and papers in the gutter using fully grammatical sentences; FAIL if the passage still relies on fragment chains like "The lane. The men closing in."

[ch11_prose_001] PASS if the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:195 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:287 make Lucien's hurt and Elise's stress legible through scene behavior or spoken texture without diagnostic phrasing like "plainer," "simpler," or "flat, over-clean"; FAIL if those beats still read like voice-profile notes pasted into narration.

[ch11_prose_002] PASS if, after revision, no more than two of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:119, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:241, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:339 use the "for one [modifier] second" construction; FAIL if all three instances remain.

[FBR-003] Reading snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3677-3678 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3771-3772, a reader should immediately feel that Lucie and Abel are reacting in distinct ways. Fail if both beats still rely on the same stillness construction or if either reaction could be swapped into the other without changing the chapter.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:227, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:253, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:303 makes Ferrand's loss of physical control over the satchel visible on the page before Abel disappears; FAIL if readers still have to infer that Ferrand simply forgot the one object the chapter keeps showing him protect.
findings.problem: The chapter's decisive turn depends on Ferrand abruptly mishandling the satchel the text has already established he guards instinctively. He protects it in the street and through Nadir's arrest aftermath, then leaves gelignite and a pistol unattended long enough for Abel to steal them. That makes the end-of-chapter countdown feel arranged by authorial convenience rather than earned causality.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the satchel handoff around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:227, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:249, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:253, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:299, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:303 so Abel's access is shown and motivated. Viable fixes include forcing Ferrand to surrender the bag under room pressure, showing Abel physically lifting it during a specific distraction, or giving Ferrand an immediate need that credibly overrides the possessiveness established earlier.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:145 still conveys the lane, closing officers, waiting carriage, and papers in the gutter using fully grammatical sentences; FAIL if the passage still relies on fragment chains like "The lane. The men closing in."
findings.problem: The recap beat breaks the constitution's anti-clipped-narration rule. "Elise did. The lane. The men closing in. The carriage. The papers in the gutter." reads like note-form shorthand at the exact point the scene should stay embodied and precise.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the report beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:145 into grammatically complete compressed sentences that still preserve speed and sequence. Keep the same arrest facts, but render them as lived narration rather than article-stripped fragments.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:195 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:287 make Lucien's hurt and Elise's stress legible through scene behavior or spoken texture without diagnostic phrasing like "plainer," "simpler," or "flat, over-clean"; FAIL if those beats still read like voice-profile notes pasted into narration.
findings.problem: Two stress beats read like near-direct import from the style bible's calibration language instead of fresh scene-native prose. "Plainer. Hurt made him simpler, not softer" and "flat, over-clean" echo the stored stress-tell vocabulary so closely that the narration starts sounding annotated.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the stress beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:195 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:287 through observable action, pause, cough, diction, or bodily sensation instead of abstract labels that mirror the style-bible descriptors.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, after revision, no more than two of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:119, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:241, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:339 use the "for one [modifier] second" construction; FAIL if all three instances remain.
findings.problem: The chapter repeats the same beat construction three times: "for one [modifier] second." At this frequency it stops reading as choice and starts reading as a reusable prose hinge, flattening moments that should each have their own pressure signature.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary at least two of the beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:119, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:241, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:339 so those pauses are phrased through distinct image, syntax, or bodily response rather than the same temporal formula.

findings.acceptance_test: Reading snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3677-3678 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3771-3772, a reader should immediately feel that Lucie and Abel are reacting in distinct ways. Fail if both beats still rely on the same stillness construction or if either reaction could be swapped into the other without changing the chapter.
findings.problem: The same full-book stillness pattern recurs twice in this chapter, which makes two very different beats land with the same generic image. Lucie `had gone still` at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3677, and Abel's fingers `had gone still` at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3771. Because the pattern already recurs across six chapters, this chapter's duplicate use reads as habit, not choice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3677-3678 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3771-3772 so Lucie's fear and Abel's fixation register differently. Lucie's response can be child-specific and concrete; Abel's can show hunger for the satchel through involuntary motion, breath, or misdirected attention. This chapter should REPLACE both `gone still` instances rather than keep them, because the stronger keeper instance already exists later in chapter 14.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the contradiction that tenderness can coexist with surveillance, desire with betrayal, and ideology with vanity. Render violence, sex, illness, and poverty at the scale characters actually experience them. When an edge case appears, choose the more specific and less reputationally safe version if it remains faithful to character, period, and causality.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches bodily danger, sex, grief, class shame, or political cruelty and then retreats into summary, rewrite toward the exact physical and social fact the character cannot evade.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: Moderate to high; contractions are the spoken default, with full forms reserved for period formality, deliberate emphasis, or Vautrin's cultivated precision.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressive surveillance chapters with slower communal or reflective chapters; vary scene count, dialogue density, and whether the turn comes from action, information, dread, or aftermath so no two adjacent chapters run on the same structural rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Historically grounded and unsentimental; plain enough for labor and bureaucracy, lyrical only when perception earns it rather than rhetoric.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third centered on Elise, elastic enough to register class texture, political pressure, and the quarry's collective life without drifting into authorial essay.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, image-bearing sentences broken by hard factual beats at moments of violence, lust, bureaucratic coercion, or shame.
